column1: 2026-01-24|The Readings shown above follow the normal sequence
column2:
but on this occasion the appointed Gospel for the Liturgy is that for the Fourteenth Sunday of Luke which would not otherwise be used in this liturgical year

Luke 18:35-43
At that time
column4: a blind man was sitting at the roadside begging
column5: and hearing a crowd going by he asked what this was. When they told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by
column10: have mercy on me.’ Those at the front cautioned him to be silent
column11: but he roared out all the more
column13: have mercy on me.’ Jesus stopped and ordered that he be brought to him
column14: and as he approached he asked him
column15: ‘What do you want me to do for you?’ And he replied
column17: that I may recover my sight.’ Jesus said to him
column18: ‘Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you.’ He recovered his sight instantly and followed him
column19: glorifying God. And all the people watching offered praise to God.
